title: tasks[].depends_on inline FK array — third instance of FGAP-040/041 DEC-0013 violation pattern; substrate-wide audit needed
description: tasks schema declares depends_on: array<string> on each task item; TASK-040 carries depends_on: ['TASK-039']; multiple tasks across .project/tasks.json carry such inline FK arrays. Per DEC-0013 closure-table-only-for-inter-item-relationships, depends_on inline arrays are forbidden — references must live as relations.json edges with relation_type=depends_on. Same root pattern as FGAP-040 (tasks[].phase free-text label) + FGAP-041 (issues[].resolved_by free-text). Third instance triggers a substrate-wide audit obligation: every existing schema's array<string> field that references other block items is a DEC-0013 candidate violation. Surfaced by constrained-harness LLM evaluation.

evidence.reference: tasks[].depends_on declared as array<string> — inline FK array shape
TASK-040 + others carry depends_on values referencing other TASK-NNN items as inline strings rather than closure-table edges
FGAP-040 + FGAP-041 same root pattern; FGAP-046 is the third explicit instance
DEC-0013 — closure-table is canonical primitive for ALL inter-item relationships; FK-as-field forbidden; no carve-out for depends_on

impact: Substrate-wide DEC-0013 violation across tasks.json (multiple items carrying depends_on inline) and potentially other schemas. Cross-cutting pattern observation: the substrate audit must enumerate every array<string> field on every schema and classify each as (a) genuine inline data field (not an inter-item reference) OR (b) DEC-0013-violating FK array requiring migration to closure-table. Per pattern in FGAP-040/041 the migration vehicle is Phase 5 (TASK-025 / arc-tracking-blocks dogfood + schema-migrations.ts) — the same migration arc that lands relations.json + config.relation_types[].
proposed_resolution: (1) Substrate-wide audit of every registry schema's array<string> fields; classify each as data-field OR FK-array. (2) For each FK-array: migration to relations.json closure-table edges with declared relation_type (depends_on / related / etc.) registered in config.relation_types[]. (3) Schema modifications via schema-migrations.ts version bumps removing the inline array. (4) Existing block items: per-item migration writing the inline values as relations.json edges + removing the inline field. (5) Migration scope tied to Phase 5 + 6 of FGAP-026 closure; FGAP-040/041/046 + future-discovered instances share the same migration arc — file each surfaced instance against this canonical pattern; do not relitigate per-field.
